_comment: RAT runtime/v1 conformance vectors — ADR-003 shared golden data. The SINGLE source of truth for the runtime axis. Both independent references (Go: examples/runtime/inmemory-go, Python: examples/runtime/inmemory-py) load THIS file and drive RuntimeService.Execute over real gRPC. runtime/v1 is the FIRST streaming axis: Execute returns a stream of ExecuteResponse (a progress|completed oneof) — interim ExecuteProgress liveness updates then a terminal ExecuteCompleted. The references run a tiny work_spec (JSON {steps, rows, indeterminate, fail}): they emit `steps` progress messages (fraction (i+1)/steps, or ABSENT when indeterminate — exercising the proto3 optional double presence) then a terminal completion carrying success + WriteResult.rows_affected, or success=false+error when `fail` is set. The point under test is the STREAMING wire contract (progress framing, optional-fraction presence, terminal oneof), not real compute. NOTE: runtime is driven DIRECTLY (not through the stub core gateway) because the gateway's CapabilityInvokeService.Invoke is unary-only and cannot mediate a server-streaming capability — a contract finding surfaced by this axis (see ideas/inbox.md / a candidate streaming-invoke ADR).
